sections.title: 1. Purpose
sections.section_prompt: State that this procedure defines how {company_legal_name} evaluates, selects, qualifies, re-evaluates, and (if needed) disqualifies suppliers of products and services that affect medical device quality, per ISO 13485:2016 §7.4.1 and 21 CFR 820.50. Reference the Approved Supplier List {ref:SC-001}, evaluation report {ref:SC-003}, monitoring {ref:SC-005}, and SCAR process {ref:SC-006}.

sections.title: 2. Scope
sections.content: Applies to all external providers of materials, components, sterile services, contract manufacturing, calibration, software (incl. SOUP), and consultancy that impact conformity to product requirements at {company_legal_name}. Excludes office supplies and other non-quality-impacting purchases.

sections.title: 3. Supplier Classification (Criticality)
clauses: 3.1 Critical — supplies direct product, sterile service, software/SOUP, or service whose failure can affect safety/performance or regulatory conformity
3.2 Major — supplies indirect product or service affecting QMS outputs (e.g. calibration, validated IT)
3.3 Minor — supplies items with no direct impact on product quality or QMS records
sections.section_prompt: For each class, define qualification depth (questionnaire only / questionnaire + audit / on-site audit) and re-evaluation frequency. Cross-reference risk file {ref:RD-007} for risk-based controls on critical suppliers.

sections.title: 4. Assessment Criteria Matrix
sections.ai_rows_prompt: Generate 10–12 weighted assessment criteria covering: QMS certification (ISO 13485/9001), regulatory status (FDA registration, CE, MDSAP), technical capability, capacity, financial stability, on-time delivery history, quality history (PPM/RMA), change-notification practice, sub-tier control, cybersecurity posture (for software/SOUP), conflict minerals / REACH / RoHS, business continuity. Weights must sum to 100. Return JSON array.

sections.title: 5. Qualification Process
clauses: 5.4 Risk-based audit decision — desk audit, remote audit, or on-site per {ref:SC-007}
5.5 Sample / first-article evaluation for critical components
5.6 Quality Agreement and Purchase Controls established
5.7 Approval by QA Director ({qa_manager_name}) and entry into {ref:SC-001}
5.8 Conditional approval pathway (with mandatory CAPA per {ref:CA-001})
sections.section_prompt: For each step, define inputs, outputs, responsible role, and timeline. Make clear that critical suppliers cannot be released for first PO until 5.5 and 5.6 are complete.

sections.title: 6. Audit & Re-evaluation Triggers
sections.ai_rows_prompt: Generate 8–10 audit/re-evaluation triggers, each with the required action, timeline, and the QMS record it feeds: (1) scheduled periodic re-evaluation (critical=annual, major=biennial, minor=triennial → update {ref:SC-005}); (2) two consecutive lot rejections or PPM > threshold → issue SCAR {ref:SC-006}; (3) supplier-initiated change notification → change assessment {ref:RA-008}; (4) regulatory action against the supplier (FDA 483, warning letter, NB finding) → for-cause audit {ref:SC-007}; (5) post-market signal or complaint trend implicating supplied component → CAPA {ref:CA-001}; (6) ownership change / site relocation → re-qualification; (7) cybersecurity incident at software/SOUP supplier → risk re-assessment {ref:RD-007}; (8) failed surveillance audit; (9) recall or FSCA on supplier product; (10) loss of certification. Return JSON array.

sections.title: 7. Traceability to Corrective Action
sections.section_prompt: Describe the closed loop: nonconforming supplier material → NCR {ref:CA-007} → SCAR {ref:SC-006} → CAPA {ref:CA-001}/{ref:CA-002} → effectiveness check {ref:CA-008} → update supplier scorecard {ref:SC-005} → if recurrent, downgrade or disqualify in {ref:SC-001}. Make clear that every SCAR opened against a critical supplier MUST spawn a CAPA, and that escaped product triggers risk file re-assessment {ref:RD-007} and, where reportable, vigilance {ref:CA-005}.

sections.title: 8. Disqualification & Transition
sections.section_prompt: Define grounds for disqualification (failed CAPA effectiveness, loss of certification, repeated major nonconformity, ethical/regulatory breach), the approval authority, transition plan to alternate source, last-time-buy controls, and notification flow to Regulatory Affairs for change assessment {ref:RA-008}.

sections.title: 9. Roles & Responsibilities
sections.section_prompt: Describe responsibilities: SCM Lead ({scm_lead_name}) owns sourcing and scorecards; QA Manager ({qa_manager_name}) owns qualification decisions and SCAR closure; QA Director approves critical-supplier addition/removal; Engineering supports first-article and change assessments.

sections.title: 10. Records & Retention
sections.section_prompt: List records: SAQ, qualification report {ref:SC-003}, quality agreement, audit report {ref:SC-007}, scorecard {ref:SC-005}, SCAR {ref:SC-006}, ASL {ref:SC-001}. State retention per QP-004 (life of device + 10 years).
